zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Welby於1340年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾維爾比時，他的父親Richard Welby22歲，母親Elizabeth Meeres of Kirton-18歲。他於1354年之前與Beatrice Mayson在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾莫爾頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1387年在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾莫爾頓享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾莫爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Welby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places called Welby, one in Leicestershire and one in Lincolnshire. The Lincolnshire placename derives from Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’ + Old Norse bý ‘farmstead, village’. The Leicestershire placename is recorded as Alebi in 1086 and Welby in 1518, and derives from the Old Norse personal name Áli + Old Norse bý. The name is established in Ireland (Galway).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Welby was born in 1340, in Welby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Welby, was 22 and his mother, Elizabeth Meeres of Kirton, was -18. He married Beatrice Mayson before 1354, in Moulton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1387, in Moulton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47, and was buried in Moulton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de Welby est né en 1340, à Welby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Welby, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Meeres of Kirton, avait -18 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice Mayson avant 1354, à Moulton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1387, à Moulton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Moulton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de Welby 1340 in Welby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Welby 22 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Meeres of Kirton -18. Er starb 1387 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Moulton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Moulton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Welby è nato il nel 1340 nel Welby, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard Welby, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Meeres of Kirton, aveva -18 anni. Morì nel 1387, nel Moulton, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Moulton, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Welby(이)가 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 웰비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Welby(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Meeres of Kirton(은)는 -18세였다. 그는 1354년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 모우턴에서 Beatrice Mayson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1387년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 모우턴에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 모우턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Welby родился в 1340 г. в местности велби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Welby было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Meeres of Kirton, -18. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice Mayson перед 1354 г. в местности моултон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1387 г. в местности моултон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности моултон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.
